new_bullets: when asked "Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6212774", you must search for heart rate observations in two distinct date ranges (past 6 hours and past 12 hours) relative to the current time, compute the numeric average separately for each period, and report both as distinct numeric values (e.g., "79.75 and 80.8")—do not return the same value for both periods, as the 12-hour window includes more observations and will produce a different average than the 6-hour window.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6540602 has a matching naloxone prescription," first search for all active opioid orders using the specific opioid medication names (hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) for that patient; if no active opioid orders are found, explicitly state that no active opioid orders were found and that therefore no naloxone is required, and do not simply report that no active opioid orders exist without first performing the actual opioid-specific search for that specific patient.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first search for heart rate observations using the correct code (e.g., 8867-4 or HEARTRATE) and the appropriate date ranges relative to the current time, then compute and report the numeric average for each time period—do not search for other vital signs like BP, SpO2, or Temp instead of heart rate, and do not simply report that no heart rate data is available without first performing the actual search for heart rate observations in both date ranges.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2748981. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.", you must first search for the procedure using the correct CPT codes (IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001) to retrieve the actual study date; if a procedure is found, compare its date to the current date to determine if more than 12 months have passed and order a new study only if it has, but if no procedure is found, report that no procedure was found and explicitly state that no new study will be ordered — do not simply report "No CT Abdomen procedure found" without also explicitly stating that no new study will be ordered.

when asked "Check if patient S2380121 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", first search for the catheter insertion procedure using the correct code (e.g., NUR1373) to get the most recent insertion date and time, then retrieve the actual current time from the system rather than assuming the insertion date is in the future based on a misreading of the timestamp; if the insertion date is actually in the past and the duration exceeds 48 hours, you must then search for a documented removal order before creating a removal service request.
